sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Irritable bowel syndrome with functional abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports a history of irritable bowel syndrome with functional abdominal pain, accompanied by nausea and vomiting. She was previously diagnosed with irritable bowel in 2019 and treated with bentyl. She reports a previous prescription for reglan for nausea, which she used as needed. She has been instructed to start a low dose fiber supplement, such as citrucel, probiotics to help with gas and bloating, bentyl up to four times daily for intestinal cramping, and stop reglan and use zofran as needed. She reports going out to eat some on friday nights and saturday nights and that she has had some spicy foods or spicy shrimp. She has been getting both nausea and abdominal pain. She has been experiencing cramping and sharp pains, so she has been avoiding heavy foods. She has noticed that bowel movements are between two and three times a day and they are formed. She has been drinking water instead of sodas and has noticed it helps. She has been avoiding greasy or fatty foods and sugary drinks. She has been using bentyl 20 milligrams as needed 30 to 45 minutes prior to eating out at a restaurant.
Findings

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Examination reveals the patient to be alert and cooperative, appearing stated age. The patient's heart rhythm is regular and rate is appropriate. Lung auscultation is clear bilaterally. The abdomen is soft and non-tender, with no masses or organomegaly. Normal active bowel sounds are present.
Assessment
The patient presents with irritable bowel syndrome, characterized by abdominal pain, nausea, and altered bowel habits.
Plan
I have encouraged dietary and lifestyle modifications, as well as starting a neuromodulator such as elavil 25 milligrams at bedtime for ibs. I have ordered a prescription for citrucel, to be taken twice daily for regularity. I have also ordered a prescription for bentyl 20 milligrams, to be taken as needed 30 to 45 minutes prior to eating out at a restaurant. I have instructed the patient to continue zofran as needed. I have scheduled a follow-up appointment in one month by emailing over mychart regarding any side effects and issues, or issues with elavil or if adjustments are needed.
